asset_type: TaraHome technical blog article cover and library card
prompt: Create a clean editorial photograph about improving a Home Assistant Thread radio channel and reducing 2.4 GHz interference in a real home. Use a warm, lived-in residential entryway or living-room console made of natural oak, with soft plaster walls and one restrained dark-forest-green accent. In the centered foreground, show a homeowner's hand gently moving a small unbranded smart-home USB radio adapter on a normal extension cable farther away from an unbranded home Wi-Fi router and compact home-automation computer. The devices should be plausible generic categories, not copies of or invented versions of any named commercial model. Communicate better physical spacing without graphic annotation. Use photorealistic editorial residential photography, natural everyday texture, soft late-morning window light, and a wide landscape composition whose focal action works in both 16:10 and 16:9 crops. No words, numbers, labels, logos, brand marks, screens, QR codes, watermarks, posters, diagrams, charts, network paths, signal waves, routing lines, decorative overlays, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, fake app interfaces, futuristic hardware, or embedded headline.
